name: Texas BBQ Beef Brisket
recipeIngredient: Oak, pecan, mesquite, or hickory chips -- soaked 30 minutes
1 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare briquets; add desired wood chips.
2. Trim excess fat from beef brisket.
3. Combine paprika and half a teaspoon of black pepper; rub evenly over the surface of the brisket.
4. Place brisket, fat side down, in an 11 1/2 x 9-inch disposable aluminum pan.
5. Add one cup of water. Cover pan tightly with aluminum foil. Place in the center of the grill over very low coals.
6. Cook for 5 hours, turning brisket over every 1 and 1/2 hours; remove excess fat from the pan as it accumulates. Add additional half a cup of water to the pan if needed. Periodically add just enough additional briquets to keep the coals at very low temperature.
7. Remove brisket from the pan; reserve the pan drippings.
8. Place brisket on the grill, fat side down, directly over very low coals.
9. Replace grill cover and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
10. Meanwhile, skim and discard fat from pan drippings; reserve one cup of drippings.
11. Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add onions; cook until tender-crisp, stirring occasionally. Add reserved pan drippings, remaining half a teaspoon of black pepper, catsup, lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce; simmer for approximately 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
12. Trim excess fat from brisket; carve brisket across the grain into thin slices.
